items.title: University Mission to Dakar for Psychology Education in Senegal
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 16 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Dakar, Michele Tommasi, met with a delegation from the “G. d’Annunzio” University of Chieti-Pescara, which was on an institutional mission to Senegal as part of the preparation of an Erasmus+ Capacity Building in Higher Education project proposal. The mission, which involves Italian, French, and Senegalese partners, aims to strengthen university and scientific cooperation in the field of psychology in Senegal, with a particular focus on the development of education and research. The project is coordinated by Université Paris Cité (France) a
items.titleIt: University Mission to Dakar for Psychology Education in Senegal

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: Ol Kalou By-Election - Security Chiefs Say Poll Peaceful, Reject Intimidation Claims
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Security chiefs overseeing the Ol Kalou parliamentary by-election have assured voters the exercise is proceeding peacefully, dismissing allegations of intimidation and saying no major security incidents have been reported despite accusations traded by rival political camps.

items.title: Sanctions on Moscow won't stop war, hope there aren't another 20 packages - Salvini
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 16 - Right-wing League party leader, Deputy Premier and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini said Thursday that European Union sanctions on Moscow would not stop the UKraine war and he hoped there would not be another 20 packages of them. "It doesn't seem to me that the sanctions have brought an end to the conflict... and I hope there aren't another 20 sanctions packages before we reach the end of this war. At the negotiating table, because this conflict won't end with a clear winner," Salvini said this during a video conference at a meeting of Gim Unimpresa, the association of
items.titleIt: Sanctions on Moscow won't stop war, hope there aren't another 20 packages - Salvini

items.title: Italy-Japan Innovation Forum in Milan
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 15 LUG - The roundtable discussion “Lombardy and Japan: Shaping Innovation Together,” organized by the Lombardy Region, Assolombarda, Promos Italia, and JETRO Milan, was held in Milan at the Belvedere of Palazzo Lombardia. The initiative brought together a select delegation of Japanese companies with the aim of exploring opportunities for industrial and commercial collaboration with innovative Italian startups and SMEs active in international markets. Discussions focused in particular on the life sciences and renewable energy sectors, identified as strategic areas for developing
items.titleIt: Italy-Japan Innovation Forum in Milan

items.title: EU report: Agri' markets expected to remain resilient in 2026 despite heat risks
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 15 - EU agricultural markets are expected to remain resilient in 2026 despite uncertainties linked to the conflict in the Middle East, although spring and summer crops could be affected by exceptionally high temperatures and water shortages, particularly in drought-prone regions, according to the European Commission's summer edition of its short-term outlook for EU agricultural markets. The Commission noted that the report does not take into account the current heatwaves, as the underlying data were collected before they began. The analysis highlights that the El Niño climat
items.titleIt: EU report: Agri' markets expected to remain resilient in 2026 despite heat risks

items.title: Markets may be underestimating risks, sharp correction possible - BoI's Panetta
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 15 - Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta said Wednesday that the financial markets may be underestimating the risks the global economy faces and a major corrections could be in store. "The speed of the market recovery (following shock of the Iran war) should be interpreted with caution," Panetta said during a speech at the assembly of Italian banking association ABI. "It may indicate confidence in the global economy's ability to absorb these tensions. "However, it may also signal an underestimation of the risks, a hypothesis that is supported by various indicators. "The ris
items.titleIt: Markets may be underestimating risks, sharp correction possible - BoI's Panetta

items.title: Villa warned over sportswashing after Visit Rwanda deal
items.description: Aston Villa are warned their new £20m-a-year sponsorship deal with Visit Rwanda will be used by the nation to sportswash its human rights record.

items.title: Sudan: Sudan Cotton Company Director Reviews Progress of Post-War Rehabilitation Work
items.description: [SNA] Khartoum -- Director General of the Sudan Cotton Company, Lieutenant General Dr. Nasreddin Abdel Fattah, on Thursday inspected the company's external facilities to review the progress of rehabilitation work on war-damaged assets under the company's recovery plan. During the visit, he was briefed on projects being implemented by the company's Engineering Unit in preparation for the current agricultural season and commended the progress achieved.
items.titleIt: Sudan: Sudan Cotton Company Director Reviews Progress of Post-War Rehabilitation Work

items.title: Ethiopia: More Than 10,700 Tons of Fish Harvested From Lake Created By GERD
items.description: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Over 10,700 tons of fish were harvested from Lake Nigat, a massive artificial reservoir formed by the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, in the concluded Ethiopian fiscal year, according to Benishangul-Gumuz Region Agriculture Bureau.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: More Than 10,700 Tons of Fish Harvested From Lake Created By GERD
